title: The Lord Goes Down to Jordan
subtitle: The sinless One comes to be washed among sinners

stillness: Be still, and let the road fall silent.
He goes alone, on naked feet.
Walk beside him now, and say nothing.
gospel: When twenty-nine years of his life were complete,
in those days when John was baptizing and preaching,
Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee,
and went toward the place of the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
He came to John, the greater to the lesser,
And he said to John, who was then baptizing sinners:
"I beg you, baptize me along with these."
and knowing by the Spirit that he was true God and man,
having no sin, needing no washing,
was afraid and trembled, and held him back, saying:
"Lord, I who am earthly ought to be baptized by you, the heavenly One,
who have no need; for I have need to be baptized.
And you, whose begetting is without stain, come to me to be baptized?
You are the greater, and the Lord; I am the lesser, and the servant."
behold: Look upon him diligently and devoutly,
and with all your heart suffer with him.
The Lord of the world goes alone, on naked feet,
across so long a stretch of road.
He leads with him no throng of soldiers,
He has none to go ahead to the lodgings,
none to prepare what is needed.
There are no honors here, no pomp,
such as we little worms delight to use.
Yet he, whom thousands upon thousands serve in his kingdom,
and before whom ten times a hundred thousand stand,
treading the earth with naked feet,
He emptied himself, taking the form of a servant, not of a King.
consider: He came to John, God to John, Lord to servant,
King to soldier, light to the lamp,
the sun to the morning star;
the fountain to the stream, the fullness to the drop,
the Author of Baptism to the minister of Baptism.
He needed no cleansing, but made our cleansing.
With his servants the Lord, with the guilty the Judge,
he came to be baptized,
not to be cleansed by the waters, but longing to cleanse them.
He became a servant, that he might make us kings;
he became a stranger and a pilgrim,
that he might lead us to his homeland and his kingdom.
He set the way before our eyes, that we might ascend by it.
But why do we neglect him?
Why do we not humble ourselves?
Why do we so greedily seek and hold honors and pomp,
things fleeting and empty?
O vain sons of men!
Why do we take the vain for the true, the perishing for the certain,
the temporal for the eternal,
and embrace them so eagerly?
By the touch of his most pure flesh he sanctified the waters,
that he might crush the dragon's head in the water,
blot out sins, and bury the old Adam.
prayer: Will you be baptized, Lord Jesus?
For what, or what need have you of Baptism?
Does the healthy need medicine, or the clean need cleansing?
Whence comes sin to you, that Baptism should be needful?
What stain can the Lamb without stain bear?
You are above the Son of the King, and below the Son of the King,
and nowhere do you show the royal scepter.
Show your dignity. Why alone, so lowly?
O humility of the power of Christ!
How you confound the folly of my vanity!
I know some little thing, or seem to myself to know more,
and already I cannot keep silent,
quick to speak, swift to teach, slow to hear.
And Christ kept silent so long,

latinJewel.latin: Factus est servus, ut nos reges faceret; factus est advena et peregrinus, ut nos ad patriam et regnum suum perduceret.
latinJewel.english: He became a servant, that he might make us kings; he became a stranger and a pilgrim, that he might lead us to his homeland and his kingdom.
